text: Currently 3D object detection for camera-based Bird’s-Eye-View (BEV) perception is based on projecting 2D camera features into the BEV space. While depth is an essential component in camera-based 3D detection, the depth estimates from previous works are surprisingly poor. In this paper, we propose a high-performance 3D object detector named BEVDepth, where we enforce explicit depth supervision and design a camera-aware depth estimation head to significantly improve the depth accuracy. Meanwhile, a Depth Refinement Module is introduced to eliminate the drawbacks caused by inaccurate feature unprojection. By combining it with a customized Efficient Voxel Pooling (EVP) and multi-frame fusion, BEVDepth achieves 60.9% NDS on the nuScenes test set, which is the first camera-only model to achieve over 60% NDS and is still very efficient. Our code and models will be open-sourced.
explanation: The text uses domain-specific terminology and cites a concrete result (60.9% NDS for nuScenes) that resembles a real research abstract, which leans toward human authorship. However, without verification, an AI could also generate plausible-science prose with similar details.
